name: Pureed Vegetable Soup with Broccoli Florets
recipeInstructions:
Garnish options: minced fresh parsley, a dash of paprika, minced fresh chives, grated cheese, sliced almonds, or finely diced sweet red peppers.
Place potatoes, celery, carrots, onion, garlic, stock, and seasonings in a Dutch oven or 4-5 quart saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, and lower heat. Simmer until vegetables are very tender (about 10 minutes). The potatoes must be fully cooked to thicken the soup properly.
While the soup is simmering, steam the broccoli florets separately.
Transfer the simmered vegetables and broth to a food processor and process until smooth. Return the pureed soup mixture to the pan. Stir in the steamed broccoli florets. Combine any remaining ingredients (such as parsley) in a measuring cup and add to the soup. Heat gently; do not allow the mixture to boil.
Serve with garnish, if desired. Variations include substituting 2 cups of steamed chopped carrots or peas for the broccoli, using 1 teaspoon curry powder instead of thyme and nutmeg, adding grated cheese (melted into the soup base), or adjusting liquid levels for a thinner consistency.
